summary: Backend-only testing for FinSight iteration 2. Added 8 new test cases covering /api/insights/quick (no LLM), /api/insights cache + force=true, /api/challenge (shape + milestone achievement after income bump), admin auto-promotion, and admin_grant on create-order for both plans. Plus regression tests kept (auth, expenses, insights, portfolio, MF, affiliates). Also swapped the old Razorpay-503 test to assert real-order creation with the now-configured test keys. Total 33/33 pytest cases pass.

minor.issue: `days_active` uses naive (now - created_dt).days + 1 and converts to months with /30.0, so on signup-day itself months_active is clamped to 1.0 regardless of income set time. This means a freshly registered user immediately sees expected_income_to_date == monthly_income (i.e., one month of runway) on day 0. Works for gamification, but may be misleading as a 'saved' number. Consider prorating by actual days (days/30) without the max(1.0, ...) clamp, OR rename to 'income_runway' for clarity.

Milestone email trigger (asyncio.create_task on send_email_async) fires on every GET /challenge whenever the achieved_milestones set changes OR `new_milestones` is non-empty. Since `new_milestones` is derived from DB state comparison, emails are idempotent for existing milestones — but the DB write runs on every milestone-state change even if it's the *same* set (the `achieved != list(prev_achieved)` compares list vs list ordering; set vs list mismatch could cause spurious writes). Low impact but consider `set(achieved) != prev_achieved`.

When admin is granted a plan, DB is updated but no confirmation/audit record is written to payment_transactions (only real Razorpay orders get a row). Non-blocker, but auditing admin grants would be nice.

action_items: (Optional) Clarify semantics of `days_active`/`months_active` in /api/challenge — currently rounds up to 1 month on day 0, so 'saved' is effectively 'monthly_income - spend' the moment income is set.
(Optional) Use set comparison instead of list comparison when detecting milestone changes in /api/challenge to avoid spurious DB writes.
(Optional) Log admin plan grants to payment_transactions with a marker like status='admin_grant' for audit.
critical_code_review_comments: Admin auto-promotion is implemented in 3 places: startup hook, login path, and register path — good defense in depth. All three use case-insensitive compare via is_admin_email(). Verified.

/api/insights caches by user_id with 60-min TTL; force=true bypasses correctly. The cache read parses ISO datetime of updated_at — if a prior bad row has a malformed timestamp, it silently falls through to LLM re-run (safe).
/api/insights/quick returns NO ai_summary — confirmed by test, and response size is small. Good for dashboard first-paint.
Razorpay test order creation works end-to-end (order_id starts with 'order_', amount 9900 paise for basic). Payment verify path uses HMAC-SHA256 with hmac.compare_digest — correct.
No _id leaks confirmed across 8 endpoints including /api/insights/quick and /api/challenge.
send_email_async is fire-and-forget via asyncio.create_task — correctly never blocks the request path. Missing RESEND_API_KEY is handled with a warning log (graceful).
seed_data_creation: QA user profile updated to monthly_income=50000 (needed for challenge milestone test). Admin user validated (already existed). Test added and deleted one stock holding (RELIANCE + INFY).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 33 pytest cases pass on the public preview URL. QA user monthly_income is now 50000 (was 85000 → 50000 by the challenge test). If retesting challenge, QA user already has achieved milestones (1000) stored in db.challenges; so `new_milestones` may be empty on subsequent calls. Admin user is persisted and admin-promoted. Razorpay keys are live test keys — real orders get created each test run under the QA user in the Razorpay dashboard (rzp_test_Sh39ZEJgvz7LPw).
rca of the issue: No bugs found this iteration. All iteration-2 features (quick insights, insights cache with force, challenge with milestones, admin auto-promote, admin_grant subscription, real Razorpay order creation) work as specified. No _id leaks, no 500s.
